moves.note: Exit all leveraged ETFs for safety
Exit all leveraged ETFs for safety

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the single name limit.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure and reduce overall risk.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and broad market exposure.
Buy XLF at 5% to diversify into a high-scoring, non-tech sector.
Allocate 15% to cash for capital preservation and a safety buffer.
These moves reduce leverage and concentration, aligning with capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for a modest ~1.5% monthly return, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly de-risks the portfolio by eliminating leveraged ETFs and reducing concentration. Expect lower volatility, but also limited upside potential.

moves.note: Exit SOXL, below stop, high risk

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with the single name concentration limit.
Sell SOXL entirely due to its below-stop status and high risk.
Maintain a small 2% position in SPAL, keeping leveraged ETFs within limits.
Increase CRWD to 16% to lean into a performing AI/Software holding.
Buy PANW at 5% to add a high-scoring AI/Software name with good R/R.
Allocate 10% to cash to maintain a prudent buffer for new opportunities.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5% monthly growth, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while still participating in quality tech. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns, balancing growth with prudent risk management.

moves.note: Sell SOXL, below stop, high risk
Sell SPAL, below stop, high risk
Add high-scoring chip name if triggers
Maintain small cash buffer for agility

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with the single name concentration limit.
Sell SOXL and SPAL entirely as they are below stop and high risk.
Increase CRWD to 20% to lean into a strong performing AI/Software name.
Watch NVDU for a potential 8% buy, adding exposure to a high-scoring chip name.
Buy PANW at 7% to add another high R/R AI/Software setup.
Keep a 5% cash buffer for agility and to capture new opportunities.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly growth by leaning into high-scoring setups and allowing for higher concentration. It prioritizes maximizing upside, but be aware that it comes with significantly higher risk, including larger potential drawdowns and the impact of leveraged ETF decay. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

moves.note: Exit all leveraged ETFs for safety
Exit all leveraged ETFs for safety

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and shift towards income focus.
Increase QQQI to 30% to maximize monthly distribution potential.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to lower growth exposure and prioritize income.
Trim CRWD and SMH to 5% each, reducing high-beta tech and semiconductor exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETFs and minimize risk.
Allocate 15% to cash to provide a stable buffer and further reduce volatility.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% monthly distributions, prioritizing yield and low drawdown. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta names, rotating into income-focused ETFs and broad indexes. While aiming for steady distributions, remember these are variable and NAV can still decline in market downturns; this is not a savings account.
